title: Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple
tagline: Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

heroImage.alt: Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple, Karnataka

overview.short: The Ragigudda Sree Prasanna Anjaneyaswamy Temple, commonly referred to as Ragigudda Temple or Ragigudda Anjaneya Temple is a temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman, spread across 5 acres
overview.description: The Ragigudda Sree Prasanna Anjaneyaswamy Temple, commonly referred to as Ragigudda Temple or Ragigudda Anjaneya Temple is a temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman, spread across 5 acres also having a hillock in Jayanagara 9th Block area n Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. The temple also houses a Shivalinga along with Lord Rama, Goddess Sita, Lakshmana along with the main deity of Lord Hanuman in the same precinct. The temple is on a hillock. There is a smaller temple dedicated to Lord Ganesha, Navagrahas and the Goddess Rajarajeshwari at the base of the hillock.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jayaprakash Nagar, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Jayaprakash Nagar also known as J P Nagar, is an upscale residential neighbourhood located in the south of Bangalore, India. It is named after prominent Indian leader Jayaprakash Narayan.

topPlaces.name: Ragigudda - Silk Board Flyover
topPlaces.description: Ragigudda - Silk Board Flyover is a rail-cum-road flyover in the city of Bengaluru in Karnataka, India. It is the first double decker flyover in Bengaluru, which is was built by the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited between Central Silk Board and Ragigudda metro stations as part of the Yellow Line (Rashtreeya Vidyalaya Road-Delta Electronics Bommasandra).

topPlaces.name: Ragigudda metro station
topPlaces.description: Ragigudda is an elevated metro station on the north–south corridor of the Yellow Line of Namma Metro in Bengaluru, India. Located in between Jayanagar 9th Block & J.P. Nagar 2nd & 3rd Phase, this metro station is named after the Ragigudda Anjaneya Temple.

topPlaces.name: JP Nagar 4th Phase metro station
topPlaces.description: JP Nagar 4th Phase is an upcoming elevated interchange metro station on the North-South corridor of the Pink Line and the ORR-West corridor of the Orange Line of Namma Metro in Bengaluru, India. This station is located near the popular Vega City Mall and serves the residential areas of BTM Layout and JP Nagar 3rd & 4th Phase.

topPlaces.name: Karuna Trust (India)
topPlaces.description: Karuna Trust (India) is an organization involved with integrated rural development and is affiliated to Vivekananda Girijana Kalyana Kendra. Both charities were founded by Hanumappa Sudarshan.

topPlaces.name: Masjid-e-Bilal
topPlaces.description: The Masjid-e-Bilal, also known as the Bilal Mosque and the Masjid Eidgah Bilal or the Eidgah Bilal Mosque, is one of the largest mosques of Bangalore Urban, in the state of Karnataka, India. The Turkish-style mosque on Bannerghatta Road was consecrated on 11 June 2015.

topPlaces.name: Jayanagar, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Jayanagar is a residential and premium commercial neighbourhood in Bengaluru, India. It is one of the zones of Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike.

topPlaces.name: IT for Change
topPlaces.description: IT for Change is a non-governmental organization (NGO) based in Bengaluru, India, whose goal is to build a society where digital technologies contribute to human rights, social justice, and equity. It is especially known internationally for its critical and progressive approach to technology.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (34 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ragigudda Sri Prasannaswamy Anjaneya Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jayaprakash Nagar, Bengaluru, Ragigudda - Silk Board Flyover, Ragigudda metro station, JP Nagar 4th Phase metro station, Karuna Trust (India).
